assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Explicitly states PDBs are the '#1 cause of stuck upgrades' and 'Most common cause', resolving '70% of stuck upgrade cases'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' command multiple times throughout the response

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands like 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase!=Running' and checks for terminating pods

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Does not mention capacity issues, pending pods, or resource constraints as potential causes of the stuck upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch command to temporarily relax PDBs: 'kubectl patch pdb PDB_NAME -n NAMESPACE --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE's PDB timeout mechanism or automatic force-drain behavior after 1 hour

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud command to configure surge upgrade settings with max-surge-upgrade and max-unavailable-upgrade parameters

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Does not mention admission webhooks or webhook-related issues as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Does not provide specific commands or steps to validate that the fixes are working or monitor upgrade progress

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE's POD_PDB_VIOLATION notifications or Cloud Logging/Pub/Sub monitoring capabilities
